perturbation.original_text: 1. License - Individual grants Lifeway together with its affiliates, subsidiaries, parent companies and their representatives and employees have an unlimited, perpetual, non-exclusive, worldwide and, except as set forth in Section 9, royalty-free, right to use, reuse, publish, reproduce, perform, copy, create derivative works, exhibit, broadcast, and display throughout the world the name, image and likeness of Individual in Marketing Materials (as defined below) in connection with marketing, advertising or otherwise promoting the Lifeway products and/or services and for historical reference and display purposes and other internal purposes, including without limitation, internal sales meetings. Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, Lifeway shall be under no obligation to cause the Marketing Materials to be displayed, published or performed. Individual further waives the right to inspect or approve the Marketing Materials.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. License - Individual grants Lifeway together with its affiliates, subsidiaries, parent companies and their representatives and employees have an unlimited, perpetual, exclusive, worldwide and, except as set forth in Section 9, royalty-free, right to use, reuse, publish, reproduce, perform, copy, create derivative works, exhibit, broadcast, and display throughout the world the name, image and likeness of Individual in Marketing Materials (as defined below) in connection with marketing, advertising or otherwise promoting the Lifeway products and/or services and for historical reference and display purposes and other internal purposes, including without limitation, internal sales meetings. Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, Lifeway shall be under no obligation to cause the Marketing Materials to be displayed, published or performed. Individual further waives the right to inspect or approve the Marketing Materials.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'non-exclusive' to 'exclusive' in Section 1 introduces a direct contradiction. The original agreement granted Lifeway a non-exclusive license, meaning the Individual could grant similar licenses to other entities. The modified version grants Lifeway an exclusive license, which means Individual cannot grant licenses to any other parties. This contradicts the implicit understanding that the Individual retains certain rights over her image and likeness, and introduces ambiguity for how the Royalty payment in Section 9 is justified.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Compensation (a) In consideration of the rights granted by Individual hereunder, Lifeway agrees to pay Individual a royalty (the "Royalty") equal to $0.02 for each Lifeway product or individual item sold by Lifeway during each calendar month of the Term bearing Individual's first name, last name or other identifying personal characteristics; provided, however, the Royalty will cease being paid upon the death of Individual. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, the Royalty will not exceed $50,000 in any month. All undisputed Royalty payments shall be made in U.S. dollars in cash or to the order of Individual and shall be due and payable within thirty (30) days after the end of each calendar month for sales during the previous month. (b) All amounts due under this Agreement are net of any applicable taxes, duties, fees or governmental charges, including without limitation, transfer tax, sales tax, use tax, consumption tax, value-added tax, excise tax, import tax, export tax, and custom duties (collectively "Taxes"). Accordingly, Lifeway will be solely responsible for the payment of all Taxes, together with any related liabilities (including interest and penalties), imposed upon it by law or any governmental authority for which Lifeway is primarily liable. (c) The Royalty due hereunder shall be paid to Individual at the address set forth above.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'non-exclusive' to 'exclusive' directly contradicts the underlying assumption of the royalty payment structure in Section 9. If Lifeway holds an exclusive license, it implies Individual cannot grant similar rights to others, impacting the valuation and justification of the royalty. This introduces a strong contradiction because the royalty payment suggests ongoing value derived from the Individual's rights, which is undermined if Lifeway's license is exclusive. The original text 'rights granted by Individual hereunder' implies she is granting rights that she has and can also grant to others, but the exclusitivity taken away in Section 1 makes it such that those rights do not make sense in Section 9.

perturbation.original_text: 2. Marketing Materials - Marketing Materials shall mean those marketing, sales, advertising, publicity and promotional materials produced by Lifeway or produced on Lifeway's behalf that contain Individual's biographical materials, name, nickname, likeness, voice, photograph, signature or facsimile thereof, reviews, endorsements, testimonials, demonstrations, depictions of the same, or other identifying personal characteristics made in any and all forms of media, that reflect the personal opinions and views of the Individual in connection with Lifeway products and/or services in whatever medium whether now known or hereafter created including, without limitation, websites, intranets, social media, radio and television commercials, video and audio tapes, digitally formatted computer media including but not limited to internet and CD-ROMs, product labels, product packaging, point-of-purchase materials, trade, direct mail, magazines, newspapers, coupons, free standing inserts, and posters.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Marketing Materials - Marketing Materials shall mean only those marketing, sales, advertising, publicity and promotional materials pre-approved by the Individual and produced by Lifeway or produced on Lifeway's behalf that contain Individual's biographical materials, name, nickname, likeness, voice, photograph, signature or facsimile thereof, reviews, endorsements, testimonials, demonstrations, depictions of the same, or other identifying personal characteristics made in any and all forms of media, that reflect the personal opinions and views of the Individual in connection with Lifeway products and/or services in whatever medium whether now known or hereafter created including, without limitation, websites, intranets, social media, radio and television commercials, video and audio tapes, digitally formatted computer media including but not limited to internet and CD-ROMs, product labels, product packaging, point-of-purchase materials, trade, direct mail, magazines, newspapers, coupons, free standing inserts, and posters.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'pre-approved by the Individual' in Section 2 contradicts the waiver in Section 1, where 'Individual further waives the right to inspect or approve the Marketing Materials.' This change grants the Individual approval rights that were explicitly waived. Now, the definition of marketing materials is contingent upon individual approval, while the Individual waived such right, resulting in an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. License - Individual grants Lifeway together with its affiliates, subsidiaries, parent companies and their representatives and employees have an unlimited, perpetual, non-exclusive, worldwide and, except as set forth in Section 9, royalty-free, right to use, reuse, publish, reproduce, perform, copy, create derivative works, exhibit, broadcast, and display throughout the world the name, image and likeness of Individual in Marketing Materials (as defined below) in connection with marketing, advertising or otherwise promoting the Lifeway products and/or services and for historical reference and display purposes and other internal purposes, including without limitation, internal sales meetings. Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, Lifeway shall be under no obligation to cause the Marketing Materials to be displayed, published or performed. Individual further waives the right to inspect or approve the Marketing Materials.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a requirement for 'pre-approval by the Individual' to the definition of 'Marketing Materials' in Section 2. This directly contradicts Section 1, which states, 'Individual further waives the right to inspect or approve the Marketing Materials.' The addition of a pre-approval requirement fundamentally clashes with the explicit waiver of that right, creating a direct and impactful in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Non-Competing Services - Individual agrees that, during the Term hereof, she will not render similar services for, or permit the use of her name, nickname, likeness, voice, live or recorded performance, photograph, signature or facsimile thereof, and biographical materials in advertising or publicizing in any medium for any other Kefir product, yogurt product, cheese, frozen desserts and other products that compete with products manufactured or distributed by Lifeway and its affiliates, subsidiaries and parent companies other than those products manufactured or distributed by Lifeway and its affiliates, subsidiaries, and parent companies.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Non-Competing Services - Individual agrees that, during the Term hereof, she will not render similar services for, or permit the use of her name, nickname, likeness, voice, live or recorded performance, photograph, signature or facsimile thereof, and biographical materials in advertising or publicizing in any medium for any other Kefir product, yogurt product, cheese, frozen desserts and other products that compete with products manufactured or distributed by Lifeway and its affiliates, subsidiaries and parent companies or those products previously approved by Lifeway and its affiliates, subsidiaries, and parent companies.
perturbation.explanation: The modification to Section 6, changing 'other than' to 'or' and adding 'those products previously approved by Lifeway', creates a contradiction. Previously, the Individual was prohibited from working with competitors *except* for Lifeway. The modification now prevents her from working with direct competitors *or* previously approved products, implying a broader restriction that lacks clear criteria for what constitutes 'previously approved'. This creates conflict as it may contradict Section 1, granting Lifeway rights to her likeness in perpetuity, and also introduces ambiguity because previously approved means that the individual need to be consulted, and the contract already states in section 1 where 'Individual further waives the right to inspect or approve the Marketing Materials.', causing an In-Text Contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. License - Individual grants Lifeway together with its affiliates, subsidiaries, parent companies and their representatives and employees have an unlimited, perpetual, non-exclusive, worldwide and, except as set forth in Section 9, royalty-free, right to use, reuse, publish, reproduce, perform, copy, create derivative works, exhibit, broadcast, and display throughout the world the name, image and likeness of Individual in Marketing Materials (as defined below) in connection with marketing, advertising or otherwise promoting the Lifeway products and/or services and for historical reference and display purposes and other internal purposes, including without limitation, internal sales meetings. Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, Lifeway shall be under no obligation to cause the Marketing Materials to be displayed, published or performed. Individual further waives the right to inspect or approve the Marketing Materials.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by modifying the non-compete clause. The original text prohibited the Individual from working with competitors, *except* for Lifeway. The modified text prohibits working with direct competitors *or* previously approved products, implying a broader restriction. This directly conflicts with Section 1, granting Lifeway perpetual rights to the Individual's likeness, and further contradicts the waiver of approval rights ('Individual further waives the right to inspect or approve the Marketing Materials.'). This alteration creates ambiguity and conflicts with the perpetual license granted in Section 1 and the waiver of approval, potentially hindering Lifeway's ability to use the Individual's likeness in previously approved products. Therefore it creates a clear in-text contradiction.
